  EXPLANATION ON THE EQUIPMENT:

title: Section 1 FOREWORD
text:
Section 1




FOREWORD

title: FOREWORD 1. FOREWORD
text:
FOREWORD




1. FOREWORD

We wish you all the best for your KNAUS mobile home. Your „holiday home on wheels“ has been designed and manufactured
to give you the maximum possible enjoyment.

Please read through this manual carefully before using the vehicle to ensure you get the very best out of your mobile home.
Take the time to be able to enjoy the excellent comfort and high technical standards of your mobile home in a manner that is
as relaxed as possible.

Please pay particular attention to the safety information.

In addition to this manual you should also familiarise yourself with the separate manual for the base vehicle and the
appliances fitted.

If your mobile home is fitted with optional equipment, observe the accompanying special permits and the related special
instructions.

To request maintenance and repair work and if you have any special questions, please call your nearest KNAUS dealer. The
staff at your authorised dealership will be happy to advise and assist you on all matters relating to your WEINSBERG product.

Only approved spare parts from the original manufacturer can ensure quality and a perfect fit.

We cannot fulfil our warranty obligations according to the terms of our warranty if maintenance work has been neglected or
carried out incorrectly.

Please send your completed warranty card to KNAUS TABBERT GROUP GMBH to activate the warranty.

This manual is only valid in so far as the mobile home corresponds to the specifications described within.


The mobile home specifications are particularly important
when making enquiries and ordering spare parts. Model name:

 Type designation:

 Therefore please make a note of the model, type, Year of manufacture:
 year of manufacture and the chassis number of
 your KNAUS caravan on this tablet.
 Chassis number:


You will find these details on the rating plate. This is located in front under the bonnet on the FIAT chassis and on the
passenger side on the inside frame on the RENAULT chassis.

We wish you plenty of leisure time enjoyment with your new KNAUS mobile home.



The Management




SUN-TRAVELLER
SPORT-TRAVELLER MJ 2006 • 10.2005 1-1

title: Section 2 SAFETY INSTRUCTIONS

Section 2




SAFETY INSTRUCTIONS

title: SAFETY INSTRUCTIONS 2. SAFETY INSTRUCTIONS
text:
SAFETY INSTRUCTIONS




2. SAFETY INSTRUCTIONS
This section contains safety instructions that must be followed when operating the mobile home.



 Please note in particular that we cannot accept any liability for damage and operating problems
 caused by ignoring this operating manual.




2.1 List and explanation for utilised safety markings



 WARNING:
 This safety symbol appears when operating procedures must be observed in full to
 prevent „DANGER TO PEOPLE“.




 CAUTION:
 This safety symbol appears when operating procedures must be observed in full to
 prevent „DANGER TO PROPERTY“.




 NOTE:
 All safety designations with this accentuation stand for „TECHNICAL REQUIRE-
 MENTS“ that the user must observe.


The safety designations „WARNING“ and „ATTENTION“ are presented at the beginning of the paragraph to which they
refer.

The safety designation „NOTE“ symbol is printed after the respective paragraph.




 NOTE:
 These safety designations absolutely must be observed!




SUN-TRAVELLER
SPORT-TRAVELLER MJ 2006 • 09.2005 2-1

title: SAFETY INSTRUCTIONS 2.3 Safety Devices
text:
SAFETY INSTRUCTIONS




2.3 Safety Devices
Particular attention should be paid to the safety devices fitted to your mobile home. These safety devices must be regularly
checked to ensure that they are working correctly. The mobile home must not be operated if the safety devices are malfunctioning
or not working.




 SAFETY ALWAYS COMES FIRST!




2.4 Saftey regulations for participation in traffic
 The mobile home must be officially registered.

 The driver must have a respective driver’s license.

 The mobile home is designed for a maximum speed of 120 km/h. A maximum speed of 130 km/h is shown in the vehicle
 registration document.

 In Germany the admissible top speed for mobile homes is:

 Urban areas: to 3,5 tonnes total weight .... 50 km/h
 over 3,5 tonnes total weight .... 50 km/h

 Outside urban areas: to 3,5 tonnes total weight .... 100 km/h
 over 3,5 tonnes total weight .... 80 km/h

 On motorways: to 3,5 tonnes total weight .... no speed limit
 over 3,5 tonnes total weight .... 80 km/h

 In Germany , it is basically permitted to carry persons in the living area of the mobile home during the journey in
 accordance with StVZO. However these persons may only use the seats equipped with safety belts. Seats that are not
 equipped with safety belts are not to be used while travelling. Persons facing forward in the living area of the mobile home
 while the vehicle is in motion must wear the three-point seat belts. Those persons that are sitting opposite the direction of
 travel in the living area of the mobile home must also wear a safety belt. For journeys abroad the regulations in the specific
 country are to be observed! We recommend that the safety belt be worn at all times in moving vehicles.

 Due to the vehicle height you need to be aware of the mobile home’s vulnerability to side winds during the journey.

 Note the maximum total load (see vehicle certificate, no. 15) and maximum technically permissible axle loads (no. 16).

 Distribute the payload evenly throughout the mobile home (see section 4.3ff Payload).

 During the journey all seats must be turned to face the direction of travel and locked in place.




SUN-TRAVELLER
SPORT-TRAVELLER MJ 2006 • 09.2005 2-3

title: SAFETY INSTRUCTIONS  Before beginning your journey, the support legs (accessories) are to be fold up and locked.
text:
SAFETY INSTRUCTIONS




 Before beginning your journey, the support legs (accessories) are to be fold up and locked.

 Before beginning the journey check for whether the entrance door, toilet door and bath door, the cabinet doors, drawers and
 flaps and all windows and roof lights are closed. The refrigerator door must be completely closed and blocked with the
 door latch.

 If accessories are fitted this will alter the dimensions, the total weight and the handling of the mobile home. Such
 accessories may have to be entered in the vehicle documents.

 When parking the mobile home, pull the parking brake on as far as it will go.

 Use wheel chock if parking on a slope.

 Keep the roof clear from ice and snow in the winter.

 Never use snow chains on mobile homes fitted with aluminium wheels.

 If the vehicle is transported (e.g. by train, lorry), the vehicle must be loaded in the direction of travel!




2.5 General Safety Instructions
 Always ensure there is adequate ventilation when residing in the mobile home. Forced air vents in the roof lights must
 never be obstructed (e.g. by winter mats) or sealed because of the risk of suffocation from increased CO (carbon monoxide)
 levels in the air.

 You must carry with you at all times, read and understand the individual manuals for all installed systems (e.g. gas
 heater, gas cooker, refrigerator etc.) and the base vehicle (engine, brakes etc.).

 Note the headroom of the entry door and the heights of the driver and passenger doors.

 Close all doors and windows when leaving the vehicle.

 Take care not to damage the outer skin of the mobile home!

 Avoid localised loads (e.g. kneeling) as far as possible if you need to go onto the roof of the mobile home!

 Walk only in the horizontal areas of the mobile home roof and wear soft-soled shoes (e.g. trainers or pumps)! Avoid
 walking on the roof with textured-soled footwear as any stones embedded in the soles will dent the outer skin of the
 mobile home!

 Take care when climbing onto a wet or icy mobile home roof. There is a danger of slipping or falling off!




 SUN-TRAVELLER
2-4 MJ 2006 • 09.2005 SPORT-TRAVELLER

title: SAFETY INSTRUCTIONS 2.5.1 Safety regulations for version with rear carrier systems
text:
SAFETY INSTRUCTIONS




2.5.1 Safety regulations for version with rear carrier systems



 CAUTION:
 After fitting rear carrier system always note the following:
  only carry items that are intended to be carried and are listed in the respective
 operating manual of the rear carrier!
  that the load is attached and secured to the rear carrier according to instructions!
  the admissible payload capacity of the rear carrier is not exceeded!
  that the axle load distribution and the total weight of the mobile home will be
 altered!
  that the handling and braking of the mobile home will be altered!


 Only have rear carrier systems fitted by authorised workshops for safety reasons! Your responsible KNAUS-dealer will be
 glad to inform you of an authorised workshop in your vicinity.




2.6 Towing and being towed



 CAUTION:
 To prevent severe damage to the chassis and body, the tow rope, cable or bar is to
 be mounted on the front on the intended eyelets for towing the mobile home!




 CAUTION:
 Towing other vehicles with the mobile home is only permitted if the mobile home is
 equipped with a certified trailer hitch! Otherwise, there is a danger of severe damage
 to the chassis and body.




SUN-TRAVELLER
SPORT-TRAVELLER MJ 2006 • 09.2005 2-5

title: SAFETY INSTRUCTIONS 2.7 Safety Instructions for the Gas System
text:
SAFETY INSTRUCTIONS




2.7 Safety Instructions for the Gas System



 WARNING:
 To avoid damage to persons and/or property, always ensure that any gas operated
 devices that are installed at a later point in time are equipped for an operating
 pressure of 30 Millibar [mbar].


 Any installations or modifications to the gas system must be carried out by authorised specialists!

 The LPG (liquefied petroleum gas) system is inspected by an engineer before it leaves the factory. The gas system must be
 re-inspected every 2 years and following any modifications and repairs.

 To ensure a continuous flow of air in the caravan, the forced air vents in the roof, mushroom vents and in the entry must
 never be covered!




 WARNING:
 When operating the gas cooker, violation of the following information
 will result in an acute danger of death from oxygen starvation and the
 possible production of odourless and poisonous carbon monoxide (CO)!


 When using the gas cooker you must open a roof light, a window or the doors!

 The mobile home must never be heated with radiant heaters and other appliances that take their combustion air from the
 interior of the vehicle!

 Before starting up the gas heater remove any dirt and snow from the flue and the combustion air inlets to prevent carbon
 monoxide (CO) in the exhaust gas increasing to an inadmissible level!

 The gas locker must be hermetically sealed from the interior of the mobile home and there must be a ventilation opening
 on or immediately above the floor of at least 100 cm2, which must never be covered!

 Gas bottles must be kept in the gas locker where they must be held upright and secured to prevent them from spinning round!

 Do not store current-drawing devices (e.g. batteries) and/or devices which form a source of ignition in the gas locker!

 Electrical leads may only be fed through the gas locker if insulated. Do not fasten with clips!

 Only use pressure regulators with a safety valve! Other regulators are not permitted! Connect the regulator to the gas bottle
 carefully by hand (do not use spanners, pliers or similar). The threaded fittings on the gas regulator have left-hand threads.
 Use a de-icing system for the regulator for temperatures below 5 °C (e.g. Eis-Ex).




 SUN-TRAVELLER
2-6 MJ 2006 • 09.2005 SPORT-TRAVELLER

title: SAFETY INSTRUCTIONS  When switching on gas appliances (e.g. gas cooker) which are ignited by pressing a button, it must
text:
SAFETY INSTRUCTIONS




 When switching on gas appliances (e.g. gas cooker) which are ignited by pressing a button, it must be ensured that this
 automatically returns to its original position after pressing!

 The exhaust gas pipe from the gas heater must be rising along its entire length and be fastened securely in place with
 clips and gas exhaust pipe supports if necessary!

 The flue pipe must be properly connected and sealed to the gas heater and to the flue, and must not be damaged in any
 way.

 Appliances which operate with gas must not be used while refuelling or in a garage!

 Operate the gas system only with propane, butane or a mix of the two gas types! Propane can gasify down to -32 °C,
 butane only down to 0 °C.

 The gas bottle stop valve must be closed while travelling if no appliances are in use!

 Always close the appliance gas tap when you have finished using a gas appliance!

 If the mobile home is not to be used for a long period, close the gas bottle stop valve on the gas bottle!

 The regulators and flue pipes must also be checked regularly! Inspections must be recorded on the test certificate according
 to DVGW Worksheet G 607. The operator is solely responsible for ensuring the checks are carried out!

 Observe relevant national legislation outside Germany!




 WARNING:
 If you smell gas or suspect a gas leak, proceed immediately as follows:
  Close gas bottle stop valve!
  Avoid ignition sources of any sort incl. naked flames or smoking!
  Provide ventilation through the room!
  Clear the danger area!
  Inform the campsite superintendent and the fire service if
 necessary!




 NOTE:
 The gas system must be started again by a trained specialist after being checked.




SUN-TRAVELLER
SPORT-TRAVELLER MJ 2006 • 09.2005 2-7

title: SAFETY INSTRUCTIONS 2.8 Safety regulations for the electrical system
text:
SAFETY INSTRUCTIONS




2.8 Safety regulations for the electrical system

2.8.1 Starter and auxiliary battery



 WARNING:
 墌 To prevent sparking and fire hazards, the negative cable of the starter or auxiliary
 battery is to be disconnected before the positive cable when removing.
 墌 When installing the starter or auxiliary battery, the positive cable is connected
 first and then the negative cable.
 墌 To avoid short-circuits and the fire hazards, only insulated battery clamps are
 to be used. Battery jumper cables must also be equipped with insulated battery
 clamps!




2.9 Fire Safety

2.9.1 Prevention of Fire Hazards
墌 All repairs and modifications to the electrical or LPG systems and equipment must be carried out by trained personnel!

墌 Never leave children unattended in the mobile home!

墌 Keep flammable materials away from heating and cooking appliances!

墌 Never use portable heating or cooking appliances!




2.9.2 Fire Fighting
墌 It is recommended to always carry a 1-kg dry powder fire extinguisher in the mobile home! This extinguisher must be
 approved, tested and kept within easy reach.

墌 The fire extinguisher must be checked regularly by approved trained personnel (make a note of the inspection date)!




 SUN-TRAVELLER
2-8 MJ 2006 • 09.2005 SPORT-TRAVELLER

title: SAFETY INSTRUCTIONS 2.9.3 In Case of Fire
text:
SAFETY INSTRUCTIONS




2.9.3 In Case of Fire
墌 Evacuate all vehicle occupants!

墌 Switch off the electrical power supply and disconnect from the mains!

墌 Immediately close gas bottle stop valve!

墌 Sound the alarm and call the fire service!

墌 Try to put out the fire if this is possible without risk!

墌 Be aware of the location and use of emergency exits!

墌 Keep emergency exits clear!

墌 Follow the instructions for use of the fire extinguisher!




2.10 Important Notes for the Sanitary Area



 CAUTION:
 墌 The wet cubicle is only allowed to be used for showering in conjunction with a
 shower curtain or a shower door!
 墌 The toilet cubicle is only allowed to the cleaned with a damp cloth!




2.11 Environmental Notes
墌 Collect all operating fluids (e.g. lubricants, spent oil and detergents) in suitable containers and dispose of correctly!

墌 Never dispose of wastewater of any kind or domestic refuse in the gutter or in the countryside.

墌 Always empty the wastewater tank and the Thetford unit at disposal facilities on the campsite or at specially designated
 disposal facilities. Observe town and district regulations and ask about disposal facilities.

墌 Use an environmentally friendly and biologically degradable WC chemical in small doses.

墌 Separate domestic refuse into glass, metal tins, plastic and wet refuse.




SUN-TRAVELLER
SPORT-TRAVELLER MJ 2006 • 09.2005 2-9

title: SAFETY INSTRUCTIONS 墌 Never dispose of domestic refuse in waste bins in car parks. Ask about disposal facilities in town
text:
SAFETY INSTRUCTIONS




墌 Never dispose of domestic refuse in waste bins in car parks. Ask about disposal facilities in towns and districts.

墌 If residing for a long period in a town or district, look for a specially designated parking place for mobile homes. Ask about
 parking facilities in towns or districts.

墌 Turn off the vehicle engine when parked. The engine operating temperature is reached most effectively by driving fast.




2.12 Supplementary Notes
Read the following brochures from the ADAC which form supplementary information to this handbook:

 Freies Campen und Übernachten in Europa

 Verkehrsbestimmungen für Gespanne und Wohnmobile in Europa

 Besondere Verkehrsbestimmungen für Campingfahrzeuge in Deutschland




 SUN-TRAVELLER
2-8 MJ 2006 • 09.2005 SPORT-TRAVELLER

title: Section 3 DESCRIPTION & EQUIPMENT
text:
Section 3




DESCRIPTION & EQUIPMENT

title: DESCRIPTION & EQUIPMENT 3. DESCRIPTION & EQUIPMENT
text:
DESCRIPTION & EQUIPMENT




3. DESCRIPTION & EQUIPMENT

3.1 Chassis
 A FIAT „Ducato“ or a RENAULT „Master“ chassis is used as the base vehicle for the KNAUS mobile home. You will find
 further information about the base vehicle in the separate operating instructions by the respective company.




3.2 Superstructure
 The mobile home is constructed in a „sandwich construction“ with a total thickness of up to 40 mm. The sandwich
 consists of a glued and compressed aluminium outer skin, polystyrene insulating material, wooden panelling and the
 interior wall.
 The gluing is a special feature of this type of construction. We use an adhesive which penetrates about 5 mm into both
 sides of the polystyrene, thereby cross-linking the material. The result is not just a surface join but also a homogenous
 bond between the components.
 Combined with precision workmanship, this process guarantees optimal thermal insulation.

 The double-shell GFK roof of the mobile home is constructed in a single piece vacuum filled component. The GFK cover
 coat is applied with an additional surface coating (Gel coat) during manufacture.

 The rear wall of the mobile home is also made of double-shell vacuum GFK contruction for wieght and stability optimisation.

 Another feature of the KNAUS mobile home is the wooden panelling of the side walls, both in the contouring of the mobile
 home and in all essential recesses, windows and doors. This panelling lends the sidewalls a considerably increased stability.

 The floor is constructed of so-called „multi-function flooring“ in the mobile home. This has an overall thickness of
 60mm and is insulated with styrofoam. This insulation ensures that the floor is capable of withstanding Winter condions.

 All of the equipment previously located in the interior of the mobile home and in the storage cabinets (besides the
 heating) are under the mobile home floor between the longitudinal beams of the vehicle chassis. This greatly increases
 the capacity of the storage cabinets in the mobile home since they are now located outside of the mobile home interior.




3.3 Gas locker
 The separate, lockable gas locker is situated immediately behind the driver ’s door of the mobile home. The gas locker is
 insulated and hermetically sealed from the interior of the mobile home.




SUN-TRAVELLER
SPORT-TRAVELLER MJ 2006 • 10.2005 3-1

title: DESCRIPTION & EQUIPMENT 3.4 Furniture
text:
DESCRIPTION & EQUIPMENT




3.4 Furniture
 The furnishings are manufactured entirely from high quality materials. A very special technique is used to bend curved
 furniture. Furniture sections are made with multiple-bonded, upholstered wood. This type of finish ensures that furniture
 surfaces are simple and easy to keep clean. Certain items of furniture are also made using a combination of plywood and
 solid wood.

 Particular attention is also paid to the securing of the furniture in the interior of all KNAUS mobile homes. To enable the
 absorption of correspondingly large forces, multi-laminated plywood strips are fastened to the sidewalls at certain places.
 Because these attachment points are incorporated during actual manufacture of the sidewalls they ensure a high level of
 strength.

 All cupboard and locker doors are fitted with high-quality locks to prevent unwanted opening. All door fittings used in the
 mobile home have been chosen with great care. Here again, only the very best quality is used.

 Depending on the model, the interior of the mobile home features a permanently installed rear bed and one or more seat
 modules which can be easily converted into sleeping berths. Spacious storage lockers are provided around the interior of
 the vehicle above the rear bed, the seating modules and the kitchen range. There are no storage lockers above the alcove
 bed.




3.5 Kitchen range
 The kitchen range is fitted with a cooker, a sink and a refrigerator. The kitchen range also provides comprehensive surfaces
 and storage facilities.

 There is lighting above the kitchen range in all models. Some models also have a pullout basket storage cabinet on castors
 to hold food and crockery.




3.6 Heater
 The mobile home is either equipped with hot water central heating (ALDE) or with a powerful heater with warm air
 blower and warm water supply (TRUMA). Each mobile home also has an integral wet cubicle (toilet, bath and washroom).




3.7 Fresh water and waste water
 All mobile homes have a built-in fresh water and wastewater tank. The fresh water and the waste water tanks in all Sun-
 Traveller models are insulated underneath with an insulating shell and is kept from freezing in cold weather with the warm
 air from the heater.
 Upon special request, the fresh water and the waste water tank can also be insulated and heated with a warm flow of air
 in all Sport-Traveller models.




 SUN-TRAVELLER
3-2 MJ 2006 • 10.2005 SPORT-TRAVELLER

title: Section 4 BEFORE THE JOURNEY
text:
Section 4




BEFORE THE JOURNEY

title: BEFORE THE JOURNEY 4. BEFORE THE JOURNEY
text:
BEFORE THE JOURNEY




4. BEFORE THE JOURNEY

4.1 Using the mobile home for the first time



 WARNING:
 Read all safety instructions thoroughly before using the mobile home!




 CAUTION:
  The first time you use the mobile home, check the wheel bolts/wheel nuts are
 still tight after driving approx. 50 km and if necessary retighten. The correct
 tightening torque can be found in the table in section 22.1.1.
  Then check the wheel bolts/wheel nuts for tightness at regular intervals!




4.2 Registering the Mobile Home



 CAUTION:
 Never drive the vehicle without a number plate or insurance cover!


Mobile homes are vehicles in the sense of StVZO. Mobile homes must be officially registered before they are driven on public
roads. Mobile homes may not be operated without valid insurance.

 If the mobile home is fitted with an EU sticker, no further stickers are necessary for driving abroad in Europe, for trips
 outside Europe a national sticker is also necessary.

 If the mobile home is not fitted with an EU sticker, a national sticker is required for all trips abroad.

In Germany the national sticker is to be fitted above the right rear light. In other countries the related regulations are to be
observed.

Contact your nearest KNAUS dealer about the individual formalities required.




SUN-TRAVELLER
SPORT-TRAVELLER MJ 2006 • 10.2005 4-1

title: BEFORE THE JOURNEY 4.3 Payload
text:
BEFORE THE JOURNEY




4.3 Payload



 CAUTION:
  The maximum total load (see vehicle registration documentation) and the
 maximum technically permissible axle loads (see section 22.3 Specifications and
 floor plan with heating diagram) must not be exceeded by the payload!
  There is a danger of tyres puncturing if the total admissible weight is exceeded
 and the tyre pressure is too low. The mobile home could veer out of control!
  All additional items carried beyond the information in section 22.2.2.1 Empty
 weight, reduce the possible payload!


In order not to detrimentally affect the handling of your mobile home, take care when loading that the centre of gravity of the
payload is directly above the vehicle floor or as close to the vehicle floor as possible.




 NOTE:
 The vehicle document gives only the total admissible weight, not the inherent weight
 of the mobile home. We therefore recommend that you have the vehicle weighed on
 a public weighbridge before commencing your journey.




4.3.1 Loading the Mobile Home
 Ensure that the payload is distributed evenly between the left and right-hand sides of the vehicle.

 Secure heavy items (e.g. crockery, cutlery, jars etc.) in low storage areas or on the floor, secured against sliding!

 Place lighter objects (e.g. clothes etc.) in the upper storage areas!

 When loading the mobile home with a bicycle garage, attention is to be paid to the technically permissible rear axle load!
 To fasten bicycles, use the KNAUSattachment only!

 The bicycle carrier (accessory) may be used only to carry bicycles. The maximum load of the bicycle carrier is:

 Cycle rack for 2 bicycles (rear mounting) = 50 kg Bicycle carrier PRONTO (for all
 Cycle rack for 3 bicycles (rear mounting) = 55 kg garage model) for 2 bicycles = 30 kg
 Cycle rack for 4 bicycles (rear mounting) = 60 kg Bicycle carrier SMV for 4 bicycles = 150 kg

 The maximum roof load of the mobile home is 75 kg.




 SUN-TRAVELLER
4-2 MJ 2006 • 10.2005 SPORT-TRAVELLER

title: BEFORE THE JOURNEY  Always use the foot mat provided to walk on the roof of the mobile home!
text:
BEFORE THE JOURNEY




 Always use the foot mat provided to walk on the roof of the mobile home!

 Avoid localised loads (e.g. kneeling) as far as possible if you need to go onto the roof of the mobile home!

 It is only allowed to walk on the roof of the mobile home with soft shoes (e.g. sports shoes)!
 Avoid walking on the roof with textured-soled footwear as any stones embedded in the soles will dent the outer skin of the
 mobile home!

 Take care when climbing onto a wet or icy mobile home roof. There is a danger of slipping or falling off!

 The roof bars (optional accessory) may be used to carry only roof loads, e.g. surfboard, rubber dinghies or light canoes!

 Always note the increased total height of the mobile home when the roof bars are loaded!

 Roof loads must be tied down securely, do not use bungees!

 When calculating the payload, sections 22.2.2 to 22.2.4 are to be observed!




4.4 Brakes



 WARNING:
 Any defects in the brake system must be immediately repaired by an authorised
 garage.


Ensure that the mobile home’s brake system is operating correctly before commencing any journey.

씰 Test the brakes while driving at moderate speed and ensure that...

 the vehicle’s brake system is working,

 the wheels brake evenly,

 the vehicle does not ‘pull’ to one side.




SUN-TRAVELLER
SPORT-TRAVELLER MJ 2006 • 10.2005 4-3

title: BEFORE THE JOURNEY 4.5 Tyres
text:
BEFORE THE JOURNEY




4.5 Tyres

4.5.1 Safety information concerning the tyres



 WARNING:
  Damaged tyres are a hazard and must be attended to immediately! There is a
 possibility of the driver losing control of the mobile home if a tyre goes flat.
  When the mobile home is fully laden, tyres may burst if not inflated to the correct
 pressure. You may lose control of the vehicle as a result!
  Always check tyre pressures with cold tyres.
  The minimum statutory depth of tread in the related country must be complied
 with!


 Check tyre pressures (including spare wheel!) before every journey and adjust as necessary. If you cannot avoid driving
 with low tyre pressure, you should not exceed a maximum speed of 20 km/h.

 All 4 tyres on your mobile home should be inflated to the same pressure. The correct tyre pressure is indicated in the tyre
 pressure table in section 22.1.1 Tyre pressure table.

 The profile depth of the tyres is to be checked regularly. The more worn the tread the greater the risk of aquaplaning.

 Tyres may deflate in the areas in contact with the ground over long stationary periods when tyre pressures are too low or
 tyres can gradually deflate. You should therefore inflate the tyres to 0.3 bar above the pressures shown in the table if the unit
 will be parked for any length of time.

 Inadequate tyre pressure can cause tyres to heat up excessively and damage them internally. At high speeds this can cause
 the tread to separate from the tyre and can even lead to a tyre bursting!

 Subsequently correcting the tyre pressure will not cure hidden tyre damage.

 Kerbs should only be negotiated slowly and at right angles where possible. Avoid driving over steep and sharp-edged kerbs.
 Hidden tyre damage can be caused if you drive over kerbs or sharp objects such as stones violently or at too narrow an
 angle. This damage will have a ‘delayed action’! An accident may be caused by a tyre bursting at high speed!

 Regularly check your tyres for damage, e.g. sharp objects sticking in the tyre, holes, cuts, cracks and blisters in the tyre
 wall. An object lodged in the tyre can also damage the tyre’s interior. Damage should always be examined by a qualified
 person to see whether it can be repaired. A damaged tyre must be replaced if it cannot be repaired or if a successful repair
 is in doubt! A damaged tyre can burst!

 Never use tyres if you do not know their ‘history’. All tyres age even if they are little used or not used at all. We recommend
 that you replace the tyres on your mobile home when they are 6 years old - don’t forget the spare wheel! Even the spare
 wheel should only be used in emergencies after 6 years and driven on with extreme caution!

4.7 Vehicle Lights
Before commencing any journey it is advisable to check all lights on the inside and outside of the vehicle to ensure that they

title: BEFORE THE JOURNEY 4.9 Checking the Mobile Home Before Starting the Journey
text:
BEFORE THE JOURNEY




4.9 Checking the Mobile Home Before Starting the Journey
We suggest that you check your mobile home against the following checklist before you set off:


 Comments: Date:




 Do you have all vehicle papers – including the green card – onboard, and do all passengers have their travel documents (passport, iden-
 1: tity card, child's identity card, etc.)? Check validity of travel documents in good time!

 Do you have all necessary documents (health related documentation and vaccination certificate) for any pets carried? Information on
 2: the necessary documents can be obtained from the related consulate, from the tourist office or from an automobile club.


 3: Is the gas test certificate still valid (see gas test sticker)? Check validity in good time!


 4: Do you have a complete set of spare keys (store separately) for the mobile home and any alarm?


 5: Have all the necessary servicing work and inspections been performed on the mobile home at the correct intervals?


 6: Has the permitted total weight of the mobile home been exceeded (see section 22.3)?


 7: Was loading the mobile home performed according to specifications (see section 4.3)?


 8: Are all doors, flaps, gas locker, windows, roof lights and REMI – except forced air vents – correctly closed?


 9: Have all hanging lights been removed and stored properly?


 10: Are all tables in sleeping position (see section Sleeping)?


 11: Are you sure that all containers with liquids – including those in the refrigerateur – cannot tip over?


 12: Are the gas bottles securely fastened with fastening straps in the gas locker, are the gas bottle stop valve and all gas taps closed?


 13: Is the payload securely fastened against sliding?


 14: Have all the tyre pressures (including the spare tyre) on the mobile home been checked as per section 22.1 Tyre Pressure Table?


 15: Are first-aid-kit (check for completeness!) and hazard warning triangle slowed where they are easy to reach?


 Do you have a correctly pumped up spare wheel, the corresponding tools and a suitable vehicle jack for the maximum technically
 16: permissible laden mass in case you need to change a wheel?


 17: Do all lights work on the mobile home (test!)?

title: Section 5 DURING THE JOURNEY
text:
Section 5




DURING THE JOURNEY

title: DURING THE JOURNEY 5. DURING THE JOURNEY
text:
DURING THE JOURNEY




5. DURING THE JOURNEY

5.1 Driving Your Mobile Home


 WARNING:
  It is basically permitted to carry persons in the living area of the mobile home
 during the journey in accordance with StVZO. However these persons may only
 use the seats designated for this purpose.
  Persons sitting in the living area of the mobile home and facing forward, and the
 driver and front passenger, must wear their 3-point seat belts during the journey!
  Persons seated facing the rear in the living area of the mobile home must also
 wear seat belts if seat belts are fitted!
  The mobile home is designed for a maximum speed of 120 km/h. A maximum
 speed of 130 km/h is shown in the vehicle registration document. The permitted
 maximum speed for mobile homes in Germany is shown in section 2.4 Safety
 regulations for participation in traffic.


Always drive attentively and with consideration for other road users. The basic mobile home vehicle is classed as a commercial
vehicle (small truck). You should therefore drive the mobile home in the same way as you would a light commercial vehicle.




 CAUTION:
 When driving into courtyards, tunnels etc. as well as when manoeuvring, it is important
 that you correctly judge the height, width and length of the mobile home! The dimensions
 of the mobile home are indicated in the technical data in section 22.3.

Follow these useful hints when driving your mobile home:

 Never drive faster downhill than you would uphill.

 When driving over bridges consider the side-wall and when passing or meeting other vehicles consider that the mobile
 home can influenced by wind currents. In either situation, you may have to steer farther away from the danger than usual.

 Due to the vehicle height you need to be aware of the mobile home’s vulnerability to side winds during the journey.

 Do not underestimate the length of your mobile home.

 Remember to change gear in good time.

 Avoid jerky steering movements. Should the vehicle ever start to skid, take your foot off the brake pedal and gently steer it
 back into the correct direction of travel.




SUN-TRAVELLER
SPORT-TRAVELLER MJ 2006 • 10.2005 5-1

title: DURING THE JOURNEY NOTE:
text:
DURING THE JOURNEY




 NOTE:
 If the mobile home begins to skid, do not brake, as there is less chance of steering
 the mobile home if you do!




5.2 Refuelling the Mobile Home



 CAUTION:
 Use only the intended fuel for your mobile home!




 WARNING:
  When refuelling, make sure that all gas-fired appliances in the
 living area of the mobile home are turned off!
  Fire, naked flames and smoking are strictly forbidden!
 Explosion hazard!
  It is forbidden to use a mobile phone when re-fuelling!
  You should also observe any local regulations governing the use
 of pumps at filling stations.



You will find further information about refuelling the mobile home in the separate FIAT or RENAULT manual.




 SUN-TRAVELLER

title: DURING THE JOURNEY 5.3 Changing a Wheel
text:
DURING THE JOURNEY




5.3 Changing a Wheel
The spare wheel is located under the mobile home behind the rear axle. On some models the spare wheel is mounted in the so-
called bicycle garage. You will find the toolkit and jack beneath the front passenger seat.




 WARNING:
 墌 You should only change a wheel on firm, level ground and well away from moving
 traffic.
 墌 Other road users must be made aware of your vehicle according to the national
 regulations which apply in the country you are visiting, e.g. by using the hazard
 warning triangle.
 墌 Never lie under the mobile home when it is jacked up!
 墌 The jack is provided for wheel changing only! It must never be used for carrying
 out work under the mobile home.




 CAUTION:
 Additional support legs fitted (accessories) must not be used to jack up the mobile
 home!


You will find further information about wheel changing in the separate FIAT or RENAULT manual.




5.4 Towing and being towed

5.4.1 Towing the mobile home



 CAUTION:
 To prevent severe damage to the chassis and body, the tow rope, cable or bar is to
 be mounted on the front on the intended eyelets for towing the mobile home!


Towing the mobile home is to be performed as follows:

씰 Attach the tow rope or tow bar to the front mount eyelets on the mobile home and to the respective towing equipment on the
 towing vehicle.

 CAUTION:
 Towing other vehicles with the mobile home is only permitted if the mobile home is
 equipped with a certified trailer hitch! Otherwise, there is a danger of severe damage
 to the chassis and body.


Towing with the mobile home is to be performed as follows:

씰 Attach the tow rope or tow bar to the trailer hitch on the mobile home and to the respective towing equipment on the

title: AFTER THE JOURNEY 6. AFTER THE JOURNEY
text:
AFTER THE JOURNEY




6. AFTER THE JOURNEY

6.1 Parking Brake
When parking the mobile home, apply the parking brake (handbrake) by pulling the lever to its final position especially on
inclines.




6.2 Setting Up the Mobile Home
1. Apply the parking brake as far as it will go.

2. Align the mobile home horizontally in the direction of travel (e.g. with a spirit level). Only adjust by blocking up the wheels
 as necessary.

3. Align the mobile home crosswise to the direction of travel (e.g. with a spirit level). Adjust by blocking up the wheels as
 necessary.




6.3 Setting Up the Mobile Home with the Aid of Support Legs (Accessories)

6.3.1 Extending the Support Legs
1. Fit wheel brace onto hexagonal nut and turn clockwise to release the support legs from the retaining pin and fold down.

2. Adjust support legs to the correct length by pressing the adjusting lever.

3. Continue turning the wheel brace clockwise until the support legs are fully on the ground.

4. Align the mobile home in the direction of travel and perpendicular to the direction of travel by adjusting the support legs
 individually and e.g. checking with a spirit level.




6.3.2 Retracting the Support Legs
1. Fit the wheel brace onto the hexagonal nut and turn anticlockwise to raise the support legs slightly off the ground.

2. Adjust support legs to the correct length by pressing the adjusting lever.




SUN-TRAVELLER
SPORT-TRAVELLER MJ 2006 • 10.2005 6-1

title: AFTER THE JOURNEY CAUTION:

 The hole in the ground plate on the support feet must locate fully in the retaining
 pins when clamping!


3. Now fold up the support legs, line up the hole in the ground plate on the support feet with the retaining pin and clamp the
 legs by turning the wheel brace anticlockwise.




6.4 Awning
The following points should be remembered when using the awning:

 Do not place the awning on the weather side.

 Do not over-tension the awning cover (tent fabric) - it should just be ‘straight’.

 Do not touch the awning cover in rain.

 Do not retract the awning unless it is thoroughly dry to avoid possible mould and staining (see section 19.5 Care and
 cleaning of the sun awning)

 Keep oil and grease away from the tent fabric.

 Avoid water collecting on the awning.

 Never wash the mobile home with the awning extended.

 If you do not intend to use the awning for some time, it should be thoroughly cleaned (see section 19.5 Care and cleaning
 of the sun awning) and the mechanical parts lightly greased.


To extend and retract the awning, proceed as follows:




 CAUTION:
 Support the awning on its integral awning support legs after extending to about 1
 metre.


1. Set the awning handle to the required length and fit into the awning mechanism.

2. Extend the awning by about 1 metre by turning the handle anticlockwise.

3. Open out both awning support legs and adjust to the correct length.

title: Section 7 LIVING

LIVING

title: LIVING 7. LIVING
text:
LIVING




7. LIVING

7.1 Ventilating the Mobile Home



 WARNING:
 The integrated safety ventilation openings (forced ventilation in the
 roof lights, mushroom vents) must not be obstructed under any
 circumstances! An increased level of carbon monoxide (CO) in the
 air can be fatal!


 Providing adequate and correct ventilation for your mobile home is the best way of ensuring optimum conditions of living
 comfort.

 To ensure a continuous flow of air in the mobile home, the forced air vents in the roof, mushroom vents and in the entry
 must never be covered!

 Cooking and wet clothing releases water vapour.

 Every person releases up to 35 grams of water into the atmosphere each hour just by breathing. This is why your mobile
 home must be ventilated with the windows and roof lights depending on the level of humidity.

You will find more hints in Chapter 17. Winter Camping.




 NOTE:
 Condensation can form under the upholstery during the night. Cushions (expanded
 foam) should be placed upright and aired to remove any moisture.


Condensation may also form inside the acrylic glass double-glazing units in extreme weather conditions. This process is quite
normal and can be explained as follows:

Acrylic glass - the material from which the windows are made - is an organic material and therefore porous, i.e. moisture and/
or gases can pass through it.

With a double acrylic glass panel, extreme weather can change the air conditions in the gap between the two sheets of glass
with the result that different conditions bring about a state of equilibrium.
Put simply, this means that the air between the two panes tries to match the conditions of the atmosphere surrounding it. If the
surrounding atmosphere is continually humid then the space between the two panes of glass also tends to become more humid.

This process can be reversed - in dry conditions the air in the gap also tends to become drier.
As the temperature rises again the condensation evaporates and the glass demists.




SUN-TRAVELLER

text:

7.8 Electronic roof window REMIstar



 CAUTION:
  Do not open roof window in heavy wind, rain, hail, etc. or when the outdoor
 temperature is below -20 °C!
  Close and lock roof window before starting your journey! Open cover and
 mosquito blind (normal position)!
  Do not tread on the acrylic glass dome!
  The glass dome should be closed fully and the blackout blinds opened before
 commencing your journey and when laying up the mobile home for lengthy
 periods of time!
  Contact your local Service Centre if the roof window develops any problems!
  Any snow, ice and other obstructions must be removed before opening the glass
 dome.
  Prior to opening the glass dome under trees, in garages or similar, ensure there
 is enough clearance!


Hints on the cleaning and care of the roof window are given in section 19.4 Care and cleaning of the roof window.

The roof window is available as an optional extra and has an electronic control. It makes the interior of the mobile home
lighter, provides improved ventilation and gives the living area a pleasant sense of space.

There is a cover on the inside of the roof window to act as a curtain and a mosquito blind. The cover and mosquito blind can
be used separately or together.

The bad weather position enables the glass dome to be opened approx. 15 mm in the rain so that no water can enter the interior,
and the wind cannot open the glass dome.

8. SLEEPING

8.1 Converting the seating modules for sleeping
The alcove bed, tail section bed and the bunk beds are available for use at any time. The seating modules can also be converted
to beds in just a few simple operations.




 WARNING:
  Before using the alcove bed the guard (safety net) must be pulled up and attached
 to the fixtures provided.
  Before the alcove bed is used by children you should also ensure that the children
 cannot slip through either side of the safety net and possibly fall to the floor.
  Bunk beds must be made safe with the guards provided prior to use.
  Before bunk beds are used by children you should also ensure that the children
 cannot slip through beneath the guard or get over the guard and possibly fall to
 the floor.




8.1.1 Converting the Seating Module with
 Height-Adjustable Table to a Bed
1. Release the table by moving the handle in the direction shown
 by the arrow, and lower the table as far as it will go.

 The table feet will move like a parallelogram.

2. Pull the side seat cushions into the centre. Place the back
 cushions into the spaces.

 When raised, the table will automatically engage in its initial
 position.

title: Section 9 GAS SUPPLY
text:
Section 9




GAS SUPPLY

title: GAS SUPPLY 9. GAS SUPPLY
text:
GAS SUPPLY




9. GAS SUPPLY

9.1 General Notes on the Gas Supply



 NOTE:
 The overall gas system is designed for an operating pressure of 30 millibar [mbar]!




 WARNING:
 墌 Repairs and modifications to the gas system may only be carried
 out by the authorised service centre!
 墌 It is essential to follow the safety instructions and safety
 information when using the gas system or gas appliances!
 墌 Altering the gas system or appliances yourself is prohibited and
 dangerous!
 墌 The customer may only connect the pressure regulator to the gas
 bottle. All other work must be carried out by an authorised service
 centre.
 墌 Never search for gas leaks with a cigarette lighter or other naked
 flames!

墌 The entire gas system has been designed according to the applicable regulations governing LPG appliances and fires in
 mobile homes, and has been tested and certified by an engineer.

墌 All fitted gas-fired appliances are ignition-protected, so if the flame goes out then the ignition safety system isolates the
 gas supply automatically. Despite this safety device you should always turn off the appropriate gas tap when any of the
 appliances are not in use.

墌 Should a fault ever occur, (e.g. gas consumption is excessive or you smell gas) follow the safety information and have the
 fault repaired by a service centre.

墌 Get the service centre to certify the repair or modification to the gas system in the gas test certificate (yellow test certificate)
 and always insist on a gas leak test being carried out.

墌 To ensure a continuous flow of air in the mobile home, the forced air vents in the roof, mushroom vents and in the entry
 must never be covered!




SUN-TRAVELLER
SPORT-TRAVELLER MJ 2006 • 10.2005 9-1

title: GAS SUPPLY NOTE:
text:
GAS SUPPLY




 NOTE:
 The gas system should be checked every 2 years by a gas engineer. The vehicle
 keeper is responsible for testing the gas system!




9.2 LPG
The gas-fired appliances can be operated with propane or butane or a mixture of these two LPG types. Retail stockists usually
supply the gas in 5 kg or 11 kg refillable or returnable bottles. These bottles must be kept in the gas locker. The gas locker
should be kept secure from unauthorised access at all times!




 NOTE:
 You should use pure propane for winter camping as butane will not gasify below
 freezing point.




9.3 Placing Gas Bottles in the Gas Locker



 CAUTION:
 The gas locker flap can be opened and shut by pressing lightly!


씰 Place the gas bottles upright in the gas locker and secure them firmly with the straps provided. Fit the pressure regulator to
 the gas bottle by hand (never use spanners, pliers etc.).




9.4 General Information on the Gas Consumption
The amount of gas you use will depend on how much you use the gas appliances:

 The Trumatic (heater and hot water boiler) consumes between 170 and 500 g/h depending on the weather and the interior
 temperature setting (heater) and amount of use (hot water boiler)

 The refrigerator consumes about 18 g/h

 The refrigerator consumes about 300 g/h




 SUN-TRAVELLER
9-2 MJ 2006 • 10.2005 SPORT-TRAVELLER

title: GAS SUPPLY 9.6 Outside Gas Connector
text:
GAS SUPPLY




9.6 Outside Gas Connector

 1
 CAUTION: Gas tap closed
 Before connecting other devices tot
 he outer gas connection, ensure
 that they are equipped for an
 operating pressure of 30 mbar!
 2

A gas connector is provided on the outside of the vehicle for the
use of a gas-fired barbecue etc.
 Outside Gas Connector
The illustration opposite shows this shut-off valve in the closed
position.




9.6.1 Connecting Appliances to the Outside Gas Connector



 WARNING:
 No smoking or naked flame while connecting an external gas
 appliance!




 CAUTION:
 The tap for the gas supply can only be turned when an appliance is actually
 connected, otherwise the tap handle may be damaged!


1. To connect an appliance, fit the connecting piece for the appliance into the outside gas connector, the release ring (1) clicks
 home to engage and then open the gas valve (2).

2. To decouple the appliance, close the gas tap, push the release ring in the opposite direction to that for the removal of the
 appliance and pull out the connecting piece.

9-4 MJ 2006 • 10.2005 SPORT-TRAVELLER

title: GAS SUPPLY 9.7 Changing the Gas Bottle
text:
GAS SUPPLY




9.7 Changing the Gas Bottle



 WARNING:
  The customer may only connect the pressure regulator to the gas
 bottle. All other work must be carried out by an authorised service
 centre.
  Never use a lighted or other open source of light when changing
 the gas bottle!
  There is still some gas left even in an “empty” gas bottle. For this
 reason exercise extreme caution when changing the gas bottle!
  After changing the gas bottle, it is to be checked whether there
 are any leaks!




 CAUTION:
  The gas regulator is only to be fitted and removed by hand. Do not use any tools
 for this purpose!
  When changing the gas bottle, it is to be ensured that the gas hose is in good
 condition and is laid without any kinks!



1. Close gas bottle stop valve (1).

2. Unscrew gas regulator (2) from the gas bottle by hand (caution:
 left-hand thread).
 1
3. Undo fastening strap (3) and remove empty gas bottle from
 the gas locker.

4. Place empty gas bottle in a suitable place, never leave empty
 gas bottles unsecured in the gas locker.
 2
5. Place full gas bottle in the mounting in the gas locker and
 secure with fastening strap.

6. Screw gas regulator onto the gas bottle by hand (caution: left-
 hand thread). 3

7. Open gas bottle stop valve (1).

 Changing the Gas Bottle

title: GAS SUPPLY 9.8 DuoComfort (Automatic valve for a two-bottle gas system)
text:
GAS SUPPLY




9.8 DuoComfort (Automatic valve for a two-bottle gas system)

9.8.1 Intended usage



 WARNING:
 Using the Truma-DuoComfort is not permitted in enclosed spaces /
 rooms!




 CAUTION:
 Pressure regulators and hoses must be replaced within 10 years of manufacture at
 the very latest. The user is responsible for this replacement!


The Truma-DuoComfort is an automatic valve for a two-bottle gas system.




9.8.2 Instructions for use
The Truma-DuoComfort is connected to both gas bottles with
two high-pressure hoses (with or without a safety shut-off valve 1 2 3
in case of a broken hose, etc.). 4

The gas bottle to be used can be selected manually with a turning 4
handle (1).

The view glass shows the status of the bottle being used (2).

 Green = Full bottle
 Red = Empty bottle




 1 Rotating knob for bottle selection
 2 View glass „Operational bottle status”
 3 Switching valve
 4 Pressure gauge

 Truma-DuoComfort

title: GAS SUPPLY NOTE:
text:
GAS SUPPLY




 NOTE:
  If the pressure in the bottle being used sinks to under 5 bar, the valve will switch
 over automatically and gas will be taken from the spare bottle.
  In extremely cold weather as well as high gas consumption, the bottle pressure
 can fall to under 5 bar although there is still gas in the bottle.


The bottle defined for use by the rotary knob is always emptied first. The gas pressure regulator set then automatically
switches to the previously defined reserve bottle.

When the remote display is switched on, the related indicator A (item 5, page 9-8) or B (item 6, page 9-8) goes out and
indicates an empty gas bottle.




9.8.3 Important notes on the use of the DuoComfort
 The gas bottle must always be in a vertical position and secured against falling!

 The high-pressure hose fittings are suitable for standard 3 kg, 5 kg, 11 kg and 33 kg gas bottles.

 The high-pressure hoses are to be installed so that the pressure gauge points upward (see image page 9-6).

 Do not kink or tightly bend high-pressure hoses!




9.8.4 Changing the gas bottle on the DuoComfort



 WARNING:
 When changing the gas bottles, you must be aware that there is still
 gas in an “empty” gas bottle. For this reason smoking and any form
 of naked flame are forbidden!


The Truma-DuoComfort enables an empty gas bottle to be changed without interrupting the operation of the appliances, as all
the appliance continue to be supplied from the reserve bottle.

1. Close the valve on the empty gas bottle.

2. Unscrew the high-pressure hose from the gas bottle and remove the gas bottle.

3. Set up the full gas bottle, secure it with the fastening strap and screw the high-pressure hose to the gas bottle.




SUN-TRAVELLER
SPORT-TRAVELLER MJ 2006 • 10.2005 9-7

title: GAS SUPPLY 4. Install the safety shut-off valve and press the gas-flow monitor if necessary.
text:
GAS SUPPLY




4. Install the safety shut-off valve and press the gas-flow monitor if necessary.

After the empty gas bottle has been changed, turn the knob (item 1, Page 9-6) 180 ° to switch over to the other bottle. The
original reserve bottle is now the operational bottle. The new, full gas bottle is then the reserve bottle.




 NOTE:
 When operating with only one gas bottle according to regulations, the free high-
 pressure hose must be unscrewed from the input of the DuoComfort and the input
 of the DuoComfort must then be closed off with the attached safety cap.

title: Section 10 WATER SUPPLY
text:
Section 10




WATER SUPPLY

title: WATER SUPPLY 10. WATER SUPPLY
text:
WATER SUPPLY




10. WATER SUPPLY

10.1 Fresh Water Supply



 CAUTION:
 To prevent the water pump from seizing, do not operate it without water!


All Sun-Traveller models have a fresh water tank with a capacity of 106 litres and a water pump (immersion pump) that is
supplied by the 12 V electrical system. All Sport-Traveller models have a fresh water tank with a capacity of 115 litres and a
water pump (immersion pump) that is supplied by the 12 V electrical system. The fresh water tank is fitted in the double floor
on both models.
The fresh water tank is encased in an insulating shell that is heated by a heating spur from the heater. This keeps the fresh water
tank frost-proof even in low temperatures. The heating for the fresh water tank is described in section 10.7.

The water pump is located in the fresh water tank and is placed in operation when water is drawn from the taps.




10.2 Checking the fill level in the fresh water and waster water tanks
1. Press the button (2) on the on-board control.

2. The fill level indicator (4) shows the current fill level.

 2
To check the fill level in the waste water tank, proceed as follows:

1. Press the button (3) on the on-board control.
 Check
2. The fill level indicator (4) shows the current fill level.
 11


 3 4

 0 1/2 1/1




 0 O.K. 1/1



 Onboard Control




SUN-TRAVELLER
SPORT-TRAVELLER MJ 2006 • 10.2005 10-1

title: WATER SUPPLY 10.3 Wastewater Disposal
text:
WATER SUPPLY




10.3 Wastewater Disposal



 NOTE:
 The wastewater tank should only be drained on sites with suitable
 sewage systems or specially designated disposal facilities!


Waster water is collected in a 100 litre waster water tank in all Sun-Traveller models and in a 105 litre tank in all Sport-
Traveller models. The wastewater tank is mounted under the floor and can be encased in an insulating shell that is heated by
a heating spur as an option. This keeps the wastewater tank frost-proof even in low temperatures. The heating for the waster
water tank is described in section 10.7.

The wastewater is drained using the drain valve (ball valve) fitted to the wastewater tank. A drainage hose (optional extra)
can also be attached to the drainage valve to run the waste to special disposal sites. In modern camping sites, the drainage
hose is used for connecting the waste tank and the sewer line of the camping site.




10.4 Emptying the water system
The complete water system should be drained fully if it is not to be used for a time, especially before the mobile home is laid
up for the winter.




 NOTE:
 We recommend that you change the water in the fresh water tank regularly (e.g.
 weekly) even when the tank is full, as bacteria form in the fresh water after just a
 few days and will give the water an unpleasant taste.


1. Drain fresh water tank as per section 10.5.

2. Drain wastewater tank as per section 10.6.

3. Drain the hot water heater according to section 12.1.7 (Truma) or 12.3.2 (Alde).




 SUN-TRAVELLER
10-2 MJ 2006 • 10.2005 SPORT-TRAVELLER

title: WATER SUPPLY 10.5 Emptying the Fresh Water Tank
text:
WATER SUPPLY




10.5 Emptying the Fresh Water Tank
1. Switch off the water pump using the rocker switch (item 11, page 10-1) on the on-board control.

2. Open all water taps in the mobile home.

3. Remove the cleaning cover in the fresh water tank.

4. Remove the plugs from the base of the tank.

5. Allow the fresh water tank to drain fully.

6. Check the fill level using the fill level indicator (item 4, page 10-1) and button (item 2, page 10-1) on the on-board control.

7. Push the plug back into the drain hole and replace the cleaning cover.




10.6 Emptying the Wastewater Tank
The drainage valve for the waste tank is located outside the mobile home on the underbody.




 NOTE:
 The wastewater tank should only be drained on sites with suitable
 sewage systems or specially designated disposal facilities!


1. Move the drain valve to the „OPEN“ position and drain wastewater at a suitable disposal facility.

2. Check the fill level using the fill level indicator (item 4, page 10-1) and button (item 3, page 10-1) on the on-board control.

3. Turn the valve handle to „CLOSE“. When the drain valve (ball valve) is closed a small amount of residual water escapes
 from the valve through a channel used as a frost drain. This prevents the ball valve from freezing up.




10.7 Heating the fresh water and waste water tanks
 When the fresh water tank or the waster water tank is full, the spur valve must be open for heating the tank.

씰 To prevent freezing or overheating, the heat to the tanks can be increased or decreased by regulating the warm air spur
 valve (actuating the rotary knob or the cord).




SUN-TRAVELLER
SPORT-TRAVELLER MJ 2006 • 10.2005 10-3

title: Section 11 POWER SUPPLY
text:
Section 11




POWER SUPPLY

title: POWER SUPPLY 11. POWER SUPPLY
text:
POWER SUPPLY




11. POWER SUPPLY



 CAUTION:
 The electrical system must be checked over by a qualified service
 centre at least once a year!




11.1 230 Volt power supply (mains voltage)
You can connect your mobile home to an external power supply of 230 V. This must be protected with a residual current circuit
breaker (RCCF) rated at 30 mA.




11.1.1 Making Electrical Connection Between Mobile Home and Power Point



 CAUTION:
 When using an extension reel without a thermal safety cut-out the electric cable
 must be fully unwound from the drum.


The mobile home must be connected to the 230 Volt mains with rubber insulated flexible cable „ HO7 RN-F 3G 2,5 mm2 “ or
equivalent fitted with connectors complying with „ IEC 309 “. The electric cable should not be more than 25 m long in total!


We recommend the following configuration to ensure that you
are always equipped for all types of connection:

��� Adapter cable 2.5 mm2
 Connector with ‘scraping’ earth connection ���
 Plug with ‘scraping’ earth connection


��� Cable drum with rubber insulated flexible cable ���
 HO7 RN-F 3G 2 mm2 or equivalent ���
 Socket with ‘scraping’ earth connection


��� Adapter cable 2.5 mm2
 Socket with ‘scraping’ earth connection
 Connector with ‘scraping’ earth connection Making a Mains Connection




SUN-TRAVELLER
SPORT-TRAVELLER MJ 2006 • 10.2005 11-1

title: POWER SUPPLY NOTE:
text:
POWER SUPPLY




 NOTE:
 We also recommend that you carry an adapter set so that you can connect a plug
 with ‘scraping’ earth connection on one side with the mains socket in the country
 you are visiting on the other.


 When running the cable to the power point, start from the mobile home and connect up to the power point last.

 When disconnecting the electrical cable, remove the plug from the power point end and then reel the cable in.

When the mobile home is connected to the 230-Volt power supply, both the backup battery (battery 2) and the starter battery
(battery 1) are automatically re-charged via the charger.

All 12-Volt appliances are supplied from the backup battery.




11.2 12 Volt power supply (battery voltage)
When the 230-Volt power supply is not connected or is switched off, the living area of the mobile home is supplied with 12-V
DC power from the backup battery (battery 2) whether the vehicle’s engine is running or not.

When the engine is running, the backup battery (battery 2) and the starter battery (battery 1) are both charged up by the
vehicle’s alternator.

The 12-Volt power supply can be switched on and off with the 12-VDC system main switch on the on-board control (item 1,
page 11-4).




 CAUTION:
 Running the electrical appliances in the mobile home (especially the refrigerator
 and the hot air blower as well as the optional wastewater tank heater) from the
 backup battery for too long without a 230-Volt supply and with the vehicle’s engine
 off will considerably affect the capacity of the backup battery!


The hot air blower runs continuously when the heating is on. This will considerably reduce the capacity of the backup battery
when the 230 Volt supply is not switched or not connected.




 SUN-TRAVELLER
11-2 MJ 2006 • 10.2005 SPORT-TRAVELLER

title: POWER SUPPLY 11.3 On-board control
text:
POWER SUPPLY




11.3 On-board control
The on-board control can be used to turn on and off the electrical 12-Volt appliances „water pump“ and „appliance circuits 1
and 2“ either separately with their respective rocker switches or together with the 12-Volt system main switch.
The ceiling lighting can be switched on and off with the respective direct switch in the on-board control and the switch beside
the entry door.

All electrical appliances are each protected with fuses. The fuses are located in the distributor box under the left-hand seating
module.

The fuse for the refrigerator in 12 Volt operation is located in the distributor box.

The power circuit for the water pump has a 10 A fuse in the distributor box.

The power circuit for the direct light switches the circuit on and off for the direct light.

The power circuits for appliances 1 and 2 are each equipped with a 15 A fuse located in the distributor box.

The on-board control is equipped with low-charge protection (battery monitor). The battery monitor monitors power circuits
1 and 2 and the water pump circuit and protects the auxiliary battery from damaging low discharging.
If the auxiliary battery voltage falls under 10.8 V, all the appliances that can be switched on and off with the main switch are
turned off. This situation is the indicated by the red indicator lamp lighting up.
These appliances can be turned on again for about 2 minutes by pressing the illuminated button, they will then be turned off
again automatically.
The starter battery voltage (battery 1) and the auxiliary battery voltage (battery 2) can be queried with buttons. The voltage
status is then shown in the display field by means of a 10 position, multi-coloured series of LEDs:

 Display field 1 to 2 red Battery must be charged
 Display field 3 to 4 yellow Battery is almost flat
 Display field 5 to 10 green Battery is charged sufficiently


The LEDs in battery symbol 1 (auxiliary batter) are always illuminated if the auxiliary battery is being charged in mains- or
driving-operation.
The LED in battery symbol 1 (starter battery) is illuminated if the starter battery is being charged, i.e. if either the motor is
running or batteries 1 and 2 are running in parallel.

The current fill levels for fresh water and waster water can be called up with buttons. The fill level is then shown in the display
field by means of a 10 position, multi-coloured series of LEDs:

 Display field 1 to 3 Tank 1/4 full
 Display field 1 to 5 Tank 1/2 full
 Display field 1 to 7 Tank 3/4 full

title: POWER SUPPLY 11.4 Protection of the Electrical Circuits in
text:
POWER SUPPLY




11.4 Protection of the Electrical Circuits in
 the Mobile Home
When the mobile home is connected up to an external 230 V
supply it is protected by a 13 Amp automatic circuit breaker (1).

The electrical appliances in the living area connected to the 12-
Volt power supply are protected by separate fuses in the distributor
box under the left-hand seating module. 1 2

The air conditioning (optional extra) is protected by the 13 Amp
automatic circuit breaker (2).

The box containing the automatic circuit breakers is in the
 230 V Automatic Circuit Breaker
wardrobe.




11.5 Electrical supply for appliances
The electrical supply (230 Volt - 12 Volt) to the appliances in the mobile home is divided up as follows:



 Appliance 230 V 230 V 12 V
 (Power connection) (Power connection) Backup battery
 Direct mains connection From the charger Starter battery
 12 Volt sockets

title: POWER SUPPLY 11.6 12 V supply from backup battery and charger
text:
POWER SUPPLY




11.6 12 V supply from backup battery and charger

11.6.1 Backup Battery



 WARNING:
  No maintenance work is to be performed on the maintenance-
 free gel battery! The battery would be destroyed and there is a
 danger of the battery exploding.
  The Gel battery may only be replaced with another Gel battery!


The auxiliary battery (battery 2) can be accessed through the service flap. The main battery switch and the charger are located
under the left-hand seating module.
This means that the mobile homes have their own 12 Volt power supply.

The charge of the starter battery (battery 1) and the backup battery (battery 2) can be checked by pressing the corresponding
button (item 6 or 7, page 11-4) on the LED array (item 5, page 11-4) on the on-board control.

It is advisable to charge up the auxiliary battery with the charger for at least 18 hours before use. This requires switching the
main switch on (item 1, page 11-4).
Switch off all electrical appliances while charging so as to achieve a rapid and optimum charge. Charging should be supervised.
This is done with the button (item 7, page 11-4) and the LED display (item 5, page 11-4) on the on-board control. Before
connecting up to the 230 V mains you should check the level of charge in the auxiliary battery by pressing the button on the
LED array. Once you have connected up to the 230-Volt supply, press the button again and watch the LED display. The LED
display should now light up in the green area toward „1/1“.

If it does, this indicates that the charger is working correctly. If the LED display does not change, this indicates that the charger
is not charging.
You should try and identify the cause, i.e. check the 2 A, 2.5 A and 50 A fuses behind the backup battery under the seating
module. If you are unable to find the cause, contact an appointed dealer.




 CAUTION:
 A battery that is left flat for more than 3 weeks is damaged beyond repair and
 must be replaced.


A flat battery must be charged up with the charger for at least 48 hours before use provided the battery was not flat for more
than 3 weeks.




 SUN-TRAVELLER
11-6 MJ 2006 • 10.2005 SPORT-TRAVELLER

title: POWER SUPPLY NOTE:
text:
POWER SUPPLY




 NOTE:
 The auxiliary battery is still under a load of approximately 20 to 65 mA from
 electrical consumers (heating, charger, on-board control, etc.) even after switching
 the appliances off. Therefore, the charge status of the auxiliary battery is to be
 checked regularly to prevent any damage to it!




 CAUTION:
 The hot water boiler drains automatically when the battery monitor shuts the
 power off.


The heater is protected by a battery monitor to prevent it from fully discharging the auxiliary battery.

If the main switch (item 1 page 11-4) on the on-board control is switched on, the battery monitor will switch off the heater
when the battery voltage drops to approx. 11.0 V.
If the main switch is shut off, the heating is switched off when a voltage of approx. 12.4 Volts.




 NOTE:
 The hot water boiler will drain each time the battery monitor turns off the heater.




 CAUTION:
  The auxiliary batters is to be fully charged and then switched off with the main
 switch for longer periods of storage (item 1, page 9-11).
  The fully charged battery should be disconnected and removed from the vehicle
 in winter.
  A complete discharge can damage the battery beyond repair.
  The backup battery may only be charged with a suitable charger when off the
 vehicle.


The entire 12-Volt system can be switched on and off with the main switch on the on-board control (item 1, page 11-4).

If the rocker switch is in position » «, then the 12 Volt system is switched off.
However the electrical supply to the refrigerator (WAECO) when in 12 V operation, the ceiling light and the heater remains on.




SUN-TRAVELLER
SPORT-TRAVELLER MJ 2006 • 10.2005 11-7

title: POWER SUPPLY 11.6.2 Charger
text:
POWER SUPPLY




11.6.2 Charger
The use of high-quality electronics means that the charger operates virtually without loss of power. The automatic charging
process charges the batteries gently and without harmful overloading, thereby considerably enhancing battery life.

The charger has outputs for the starter battery (Battery 1) and the backup battery (Battery 2). The charger starts to operate as
soon as it is connected to the 230 V supply.

The auxiliary battery can be charged outside the mobile home using the charger through the KNAUS adapter cable which is
available as an extra option:

씰 Remove the auxiliary battery through the service flap, unplug the charger under the seating module and take out of the
 mount.

씰 Use the adapter cable to charge the auxiliary battery in an appropriate room by connecting the charger to a 230 Volt outlet.


You will find further information about the battery charger in the separate operating instructions from the manufacturer.

title: HEATING & HOT WATER As all other appliances in the water circuit (water pump, taps etc.) are not protected by the frost
text:
HEATING & HOT WATER




As all other appliances in the water circuit (water pump, taps etc.) are not protected by the frost protection valve and the
automatic drain could be obstructed by dirt or ice, the water must be drained after the boiler is turned off if there is a risk of
frost.

You will find further information on draining the hot water boiler in the separate manual supplied by the manufacturer.




12.2 Hot water central heating
The hot water central heating including the integrated hot water heating system is located under the seating module and is
controlled with the adjacent control element. The heater can also be operated with the hot water boiler drained.

The heater (radiators) that are fed with a circulating pump with glycol fluid are distributed so that an even temperature is
achieved throughout the interior of the mobile home. When using the central heating in the mobile home, you do not have to
do without the enjoyment of a comfortable indoor temperature - similar to a living room.

The heater in the rear of the mobile home can also be included or excluded from the heating circuit. This allows you to set an
individual room temperature for the sleeping area.

In heating operation, the heater automatically switches the circulating pump on according to the temperature difference
between the set room temperature and the actual room temperature. When the desired temperature is achieved, the circulating
pump switches off automatically.

title: HEATING & HOT WATER CAUTION:
text:
HEATING & HOT WATER




 CAUTION:
 Work on the electronic control unit for the heating may only be carried out by a
 qualified and trained service engineer.


The heating has an ionising flame control, i.e. if the flame goes out, the electronic control unit attempts to light it again.

If the flame does not ignite within 10 seconds again, the solenoid shuts the gas feed off and blocks the electronic control unit.
This procedure is indicated by the red LED illuminating (Item G, page 12-6).
If the room thermostat starts heating operation, i.e. if the temperature in the mobile home is under the set temperature, the
circulating pump is started.
This starts the fluid in the heating system to circulate and cold fluid enters the heating circuit.
The operating thermostat for the heating registers the fluid temperature and the heating system warms the fluid that circulates
through the system.

If the set temperature is achieved, the circulating pump is switched off.
The operating thermostat registers that the fluid has achieved the set temperature and the heating unit is switched off.

As soon as the fluid temperature has dropped a few degrees, the heating unit is switched on again.
This ensures that heated fluid is always running through the heating circuit if the thermostat is operating the heating.




12.2.2 Heating comfort
In order to best utilise the principle of water-based heat, it is important that the heated air can circulate under the bed boxes
and behind the back cushions freely.

It is just as important that the cushions and covers do not hinder the air circulation behind the back cushions.

To achieve an individual room temperature in the rear sleeping area of the mobile home, a „bypass switch“ enables the
heating radiator to be included or excluded from the circulation system as desired.

In affected models, this is done with a shut-off valve in the clothes closet that switches the heating radiator on or off
depending on the setting:

 Control horizontal (in direction of line) = radiator off

 Control vertical (across line direction) = radiator on




 SUN-TRAVELLER
12-8 MJ 2006 • 10.2005 SPORT-TRAVELLER

title: HEATING & HOT WATER 12.2.3 Circulating pump
text:
HEATING & HOT WATER




12.2.3 Circulating pump



 CAUTION:
 Continuous operation will decrease the service life of the circulating pump! Switch
 the circulating pump off if it is not requried.


In the expansion tank, there is a 12 V operated circulating pump that circulates teh heated glycol fluid in the heating system.
The circulating pump starts by actuating the slider switch on the controller (page 12-6) in setting „I“ or „J“. Afterwards, the
room thermostat regulates the on and off switching for the circulation pump depending on heating requirements.




12.2.4 Expansion tank
The level of the glycol fluid can be monitored in the expansion tank. This should be at approx. 1 cm over the „MIN“ mark
when the heating unit is cold (see figure below).

The heating circuit is to be filled with a 40% glycol mix as is used in automobile engines. If the heating system is subjected
to temperature under -25 °C, the glycol mixture is to be increased, but never to above 50%. Before filling with new fluid,
check the actual glycol content so that the glycol concentration in the fluid mixture is not increased too much.
The glycol mixture should be changed every 2 years since important characteristics such as e.g. corrosion protection can
deteriorate over time.
The heating system is never to be allowed to sit without glycol fluid. If the fluid level in the expansion tank drops lower than
normal condensation, the entire heating system must be checked for leaks. If glycol fluid is leaking, the affected spot is to be
rinsed thoroughly with water and then dried.

To fill the glycol mixture, proceed as follows:


 1

 WARNING:
 Be careful when
 filling the glycol
 mixture.
 Fluid is poisonous!


1. Unscrew the cap nut (1) on the expansion tank and lift the
 circulating pump.

2. Slowly pour the glycol mixture into the expansion tank until
 it reaches a level approximately 1 cm over the „MIN“ mark. Hot water central heating: Expansion tank
