title: HEATING & HOT WATER CAUTION:
text:
HEATING & HOT WATER




 CAUTION:
 After filling the glycol mixture, you must bleed the air from the heating unit before
 starting again!




12.2.5 Bleeding the air from the heating system
Depending on the circulatory system installed, air pockets can be created during filling. You will notice that when there is air
in the heating system the heat only reaches about 1 m down the line although the circulating pump is running.
If the heating system has just been filled, small air bubbles can be created in the expansion tank. If the circulating pump is shut
off for a few seconds, the air bubbles will disappear under normal circumstances.
An effective method of bleeding and filling the heating system is to use a filling pump from ALDE (optional extra).


To bleed the air from the heating system, proceed as follows:

1. Switch the heating unit on, move the slider switch (page 12-6) to position „B“ – „C“ – „D“ or „F“.

2. Ensure that the circulating pump is switched off and set the slider switch (page 12-6) to position „H“.

3. Open the bleed screws for all radiators one after the other and leave them open until fluid runs out.

4. Now switch the circulating pump back on, move the slider switch (page 12-6) to position „I“ or „J“ and let it run for a few
 minutes. Check for whether the lines and the radiators are warm everywhere.

5. If these measures do not have the desired effect you may have to fill with glycol again.

Otherwise continue as follows:




 CAUTION:
 The mobile home is not to be raised using the support legs!


1. Park the mobile home on a slope or raise it with an appropriate jack so that it leans forward. Secure the mobile home
 against rolling!

2. Wait several minutes with the mobile home in this position so that the air in the heating system can rise to the top.

3. Unscrew the bleed screw at the highest point (should be determined earlier) and wait until fluid comes out.

4. Park the mobile home on a slope or raise it with an appropriate jack so that it leans backward. Secure the mobile home
 against rolling! Repeat the previously executed procedures.




 SUN-TRAVELLER
12-10 MJ 2006 • 10.2005 SPORT-TRAVELLER

title: HEATING & HOT WATER 5. Set the mobile home up so that it is level.
text:
HEATING & HOT WATER




5. Set the mobile home up so that it is level.

6. Now, switch on the circulating pump and allow it to run for a few minutes. Check for whether the lines and the radiators are
 warm everywhere.


You will find further information in the separate manual supplied by the manufacturer.




12.3 Hot water supply using the Alde heating unit
The mobile home can be equipped with a hot water heater (water boiler in ALDE heating unit). The hot water boiler can be
operated with liquid gas, with 230 V (electric cartridge) or with both simultaneously.
The 9 litre hot water boiler provides hot water within a temperature range of 40 to 70 °C.
At maximum usage, approximately 17 litres of hot water at a temperature of 40°C is possible.

The hot water boiler requires approx. 30 minutes to heat 12 litres of water from 5°C to 40°C when working with liquid gas.
If the hot water boiler is operated with 230 V, the capacity will be slightly lower. If the electric cartridge is not sufficient for
your hot water requirements, the hot water boiler can also be operated using the 230 V and liquid gas at the same time.




 CAUTION:
 Never operate the water pump for more than a very short time without water!


The switch for the water pump (11) is on the on-board control.

An operating pressure of about 1.8 bar builds up in the water
pump when the main switch is turned on. This ensures that
pressure is available when water is required.
Should the pressure fall below a certain limit while water is
being drawn (pressure drop) the nominal pressure of 1.8 bar is
automatically restored.
 Check

 11
 NOTE:
 Switch the main switch off when
 nobody is around for longer
 periods of time.

text:

COOKING & BAKING

title: COOKING & BAKING 13. COOKING & BACKING
text:
COOKING & BAKING




13. COOKING & BACKING

13.1 Using the Gas Cooker
1. Open gas bottle shut-off valve and „ ” gas shut-off valve on the valve block under the kitchen range.

2. Lift up the cover on the hob.




 WARNING:
  To ensure a continuous flow of air in the mobile home, the forced
 air vents in the roof, mushroom vents and in the entry must never
 be covered!
  When using the cooker you must open a window or roof light to
 ensure an adequate supply of oxygen to the interior of your mobi-
 le home!
  The gas cooker should never be used as a heater!
  Explosion hazard - Never allow gas to escape unburned!
  The user must be able to keep the gas lighting process in full view
 - this must not be concealed by pots etc!
  The flame safety cover must be lifted up whenever the cooker is
 in use!


3. Turn the controller for the desired burner counter clockwise to small or large flame, press and hold.

4. Light the escaping gas with suitable lighter and keep the control knob held down for another 5 to 7 seconds. The flame may
 go out if the control knob is released too soon. Repeat the operation if this happens.

5. To switch off, turn the control knob clockwise to the „  ” position.

6. If the thermocouple is bent or faulty the burner will not operate correctly and the flame will keep going out even though the
 control knob is pressed for a longer time again and again. If this happens a new thermocouple must be fitted by a qualified
 engineer.




 NOTE:
 A flame with an indistinct pattern is a sign of poor combustion, and we therefore
 recommend annual servicing by a qualified engineer.


You will find further information in the separate operating instructions supplied by the cooker manufacturer.

title: Section 16 TOILET
text:

TOILET

title: TOILET 16. TOILET
text:
TOILET




16. TOILET

16.1 Thetford Unit



 CAUTION:
 The Thetford unit should only be drained on sites with suitable sewage
 systems or specially designated disposal facilities!


1. Before using the toilet, push down the flush button briefly to allow a small amount of water into the bowl. Repeat as
 necessary. The flow of water stops when the button is released.

2. After use, push down the flush button and turn counter-clockwise at the same time. This turning motion opens the valve
 in the WC. The toilet bowl is flushed thoroughly but is economical on water.

3. After flushing, turn the flush button clockwise to close the valve again. The toilet can also be used with the valve open.




 CAUTION:
  If there is a risk of frost and the mobile home is not heated the
 Thetford unit should be drained to prevent it from freezing.
  In winter the toilet flush should not be used until the toilet cubicle
 has thoroughly warmed up otherwise the water pump in the
 Thetford flush may be damaged.
  An environmentally friendly and fully bio-degradable chemical
 WC additive should be used for the WC.
  If environmental concerns exist, the WC can also be used without
 chemical additives but the Thetford unit will require more fre-
 quent draining as a result.



You will find further information on the Thetford toilet in the separate manual supplied by the manufacturer.




SUN-TRAVELLER
SPORT-TRAVELLER MJ 2006 • 10.2005 16-1

title: Section 17 WINTER CAMPING
text:
Section 17




WINTER CAMPING

title: WINTER CAMPING 17. WINTER CAMPING
text:
WINTER CAMPING




17. WINTER CAMPING

17.1 General Notes on Winter Camping
If you plan to enjoy the freedom of camping life in winter as much as in summer, then here are some hints which you should
follow:

 After driving on roads that have been sprayed with salt, wash the underbody of the base vehicle with water.

 Find out as much as you can about weather and driving conditions before you set out and during your journey.

 Your mobile home has all-round insulation and double glazing and is perfectly ‘winter-worthy’ if used correctly. The
 water supply is inside the vehicle on all models.

 Proper ventilation is just as important in winter as it is in summer. Ski boots and other wet items will increase the level
 of moisture and condensation inside the mobile home.

 Heating is of course particularly important in winter, For this reason the heating – the heaters installed as standard are
 normally adequate – is particularly important for winter camping. Quick removal of the water vapour generated by
 cooking, wet clothing and breathing is particularly important for camping in winter. In addition to good ventilation, the
 heated air will rapidly eliminate the water vapour, as warm air can absorb much more moisture than cold air. When there
 is moisture in the air, the lower the room temperature the quicker that condensation will form if there is a slight difference
 between the temperature of the room air and the temperature on the inside wall.

 The windows, window frames and the corners of storage lockers are particularly prone to condensation depending on
 use, the number of persons, the site and the level of humidity both inside and outside the mobile home. Adequate heating
 of the mobile home with the recirculation fan switched on will improve this situation.




17.2 Additional Notes on Winter Camping
 Secure the mobile home against rolling away then release the parking brake to prevent it from freezing.

 Place all cushions in the mobile home upright to ensure that they are aired and dried.

 Use only 100 % propane in winter as butane will not gasify below freezing point. Make sure you have an adequate supply
 of gas bottles.

 Avoid the use of electrical appliances that consume a lot of power. Campsites do not have unlimited amounts of electricity.

 In cold weather the water pipes in the mobile home can freeze if the vehicle is left for long periods with the heating
 switched off.

 To prevent freezing, drain the wastewater tank with the heating off, please refer to Section 10.5.

 The backup battery (battery 2) should be kept fully charged at all times.

 While heating up the vehicle, open all cupboards, flaps and storage lockers to prevent condensation forming.




SUN-TRAVELLER
SPORT-TRAVELLER MJ 2006 • 10.2005 17-1

title: WINTER CAMPING  Avoid storing containers (bottles, cans etc.) with water, juice or other liquids in the unheated mobile
text:
WINTER CAMPING




 Avoid storing containers (bottles, cans etc.) with water, juice or other liquids in the unheated mobile home as they may
 freeze.

 All snow and ice should be carefully removed from the roof of the vehicle and from the awning; it is particularly
 important to ensure that the flue on the side wall is kept free at all times.

 Always keep the forced air vents open, and open the roof light slightly if required.

 Never run power cables on the ground - they may freeze to the soil or be damaged by snow clearing vehicles.

 Keep metal taps open in an unheated mobile home at low temperatures, even if the water system has been drained.
 Always remember to open the mixer lever in both the cold water (blue) and the hot water (red) positions and then to leave
 it in one of these opened positions.




17.3 Recommended Accessories for Winter Camping
To be prepared for any eventuality when winter camping, we recommend the following accessories:

LAYING UP




18. LAYING UP



 WARNING:
 墌 You should completely drain the water system even if you are only laying up the
 mobile home for a short period. Bacteria will grow in the fresh water after just
 a few days, making the water undrinkable. For the fresh water tank we
 recommend a disinfectant that is available from your KNAUS dealer.
 墌 After an extended lay up, the entire brake system is to be checked by an
 authorised service centre!




18.1 Temporarily Laying Up the Mobile Home
The mobile home will not suffer from being left outdoors all year round provided a number of precautions are taken.

If the mobile home is to be temporarily laid up you should follow these steps:

 Forced air in the roof lights, mushroom vents and in the entrance are to be kept open. OK: ❑
 Remove any traces of corrosion and repair minor damage to paintwork. Products suitable for repairing paintwork are available from your
 KNAUS-dealer. OK: ❑
 Thoroughly wash your mobile home and apply a paint care product. Products suitable for the care of your mobile home are available from your
 KNAUS-dealer. OK: ❑
 Grease all moving parts of the legs. OK: ❑
 Jack up mobile home by winding down the legs so that the weight is taken off the wheels. OK: ❑
 If the mobile home is not jacked up, move the vehicle once a month to prevent damage to the tyres and bearings. Secure mobile home using
 chocks. OK: ❑
 Re-fuel mobile home. In this way you can prevent corrosion damage to the fuel system. OK: ❑
 MOBILE HOME




 Release the mobile home's handbrake. OK: ❑
 If you plan to cover the mobile home with a tarpaulin etc., ensure that air can circulate over the roof, e.g. by placing light wooden battens on the
 roof. The tarpaulin or sheet should not be able to stick to the outer skin of the mobile home. OK: ❑
 Also ensure that there is adequate air circulation under the floor. OK: ❑
 Thoroughly air the mobile home every 3 weeks. OK: ❑
 Fit cover – is available – to flue. OK: ❑

title: Section 19 CLEANING & CARE
text:
Section 19




CLEANING & CARE

title: CLEANING & CARE 19. CLEANING & CARE
text:
CLEANING & CARE




19. CLEANING & CARE

19.1 Cleaning and Care of the Exterior of the Mobile Home
The outer skin of the mobile home is made from aluminium sheet. The surface of this aluminium sheet is stove enamelled with
an acrylic paint. This process - known as polyester coil coating - is a modern, environmentally friendly way of surface finis-
hing which creates high colour brilliance and is extremely durable.
To maintain the high quality of the finish you should care for the paintwork as described below:

씰 Salt, soot, bird droppings and other aggressive substances should always be removed from the paintwork of the mobile
 home immediately before they have time to leave a stain.

씰 Wash your mobile home with water regularly if you are near the sea.

You should clean your mobile home as you would a car:




 WARNING:
 Take extra caution when walking over the roof of the vehicle when
 wet or icy. There is an increased danger of slipping or falling off!




 CAUTION:
 墌 Avoid localised loads (e.g. kneeling) as far as possible if you need to go onto the
 roof of the mobile home! Walk only in the horizontal areas of the mobile home
 roof and wear soft-soled shoes (e.g. trainers or pumps)!
 墌 Avoid walking on the mobile home roof with textured-soled footwear as any
 stones embedded in the soles will dent the outer skin of the mobile home!


씰 Shampoo your mobile home with a pH neutral product (pH: 6-8). Don’t forget the roof!

씰 Now thoroughly rinse off the vehicle with water, and dry.

씰 Use only sponges, soft cloths or soft brushes. Hard tools can damage the paintwork.

씰 The mobile home should be waxed from time to time to protect and enhance its sheen. Apply the coat of wax as directed by
 the manufacturer.

씰 To freshen up old paint, the mobile home can be treated with a suitable polish. Here again, you should follow the
 manufacturer’s directions.
 Polishing can attack the paint surface of your mobile home (abrasion) so do not apply polish unless absolutely necessary.




SUN-TRAVELLER
SPORT-TRAVELLER MJ 2006 • 10.2005 19-1

title: CLEANING & CARE 씰 Plastic parts must not be treated with aggressive cleaning agents (benzene, methylated spirits, thinne
text:
CLEANING & CARE




씰 Plastic parts must not be treated with aggressive cleaning agents (benzene, methylated spirits, thinners, etc.), on this topic
 see section 19.1.1.

씰 Tar stains or other organic stains on the painted surface are best removed with benzene. We strongly advise against the use
 of other solvents!
 Insects should be softened with water and then removed with a sponge wrapped in a nylon stocking.

씰 Damage to the surface of your mobile home should be repaired immediately with refinish paint to prevent corrosion taking
 a hold.

씰 Check the condition of the underbody protection once a year. Please contact your KNAUS dealer if the underbody protection
 is damaged.

씰 After driving on roads that have been sprayed with salt, wash the underbody of the base vehicle with water.




19.1.1 Cleaning Plastic Parts on the Exterior of the Mobile Home
씰 Plastic products can be carefully cleaned using warm water with the addition of a very slightly acidic, neutral or very
 slightly alkaline cleaner.

씰 You can use low concentrations (< 2 %) of mild household cleaners dissolved in water.

씰 Abrasive agents like scouring powder are not recommended as they will scratch plastic surfaces.

씰 Very greasy or oily surfaces can be washed down with petroleum substances free from aromatic compounds such as
 ethanol. The use of organic solvents can damage the material.

씰 Plastic mouldings can be damaged by stress cracks caused by a variety of products. Other chemicals can cause the plastic
 to swell and soften. These mouldings should therefore only be exposed to contact with the solvents listed above for short
 periods (2 minutes maximum) at room temperature.




 SUN-TRAVELLER
19-2 MJ 2006 • 10.2005 SPORT-TRAVELLER

title: CLEANING & CARE 19.2 Care and Cleaning of the Inside of the Mobile Home
text:
CLEANING & CARE




19.2 Care and Cleaning of the Inside of the Mobile Home



 CAUTION:
 墌 Use only normal household care and cleaning products!
 墌 Caustic and abrasive products should not be used!
 墌 Avoid using anything that might scratch or score surfaces!


씰 The furniture is only to be cleaned with a commercially available furniture polish. Strong, powerful cleaning products
 should not be used.

씰 Clean table tops and kitchen worktops using only water with washing-up liquid or a mild household cleaner.

씰 The kitchen sink is made from stainless steel and can be cleaned with products that are available in the shops.

씰 Dry clean upholstery covers, curtains and the valances.




 CAUTION:
 The toilet cubicle is only allowed to be cleaned with a little water with a mild
 cleaner and a damp cloth!




 NOTE:
 Sunshine can cause plastic parts in the interior of the toilet cubicle to turn yellow
 over time. This is not a quality defect however.




 CAUTION:
 Never lay the carpet onto wet PVC floor covering as the carpet can stick to the
 PVC and pull the covering with it when the carpet is next taken up!


씰 The PVC floor covering is hard-wearing and easy-care. To clean it, just wet-mop and rub dry, use no wax.

씰 Never use abrasives or aggressive cleaners.




SUN-TRAVELLER
SPORT-TRAVELLER MJ 2006 • 10.2005 19-3

title: CLEANING & CARE 19.2.1 Cleaning Plastic Parts Inside the Mobile Home
text:
CLEANING & CARE




19.2.1 Cleaning Plastic Parts Inside the Mobile Home
씰 Plastic products can be carefully cleaned using warm water with the addition of a very slightly acidic, neutral or very
 slightly alkaline cleaner.

씰 You can use low concentrations (< 2 %) of mild household cleaners dissolved in water.

씰 Abrasive agents like scouring powder are not recommended as they will scratch plastic surfaces.

씰 Very greasy or oily surfaces can be washed down with petroleum substances free from aromatic compounds such as
 petroleum ether, ethanol or perchloroethylene. The use of organic solvents can damage the material.

씰 Plastic mouldings can be damaged by stress cracks caused by a variety of products. Other chemicals can cause the plastic
 to swell and soften. These mouldings should therefore only be exposed to contact with the solvents listed above for short
 periods (5 minutes maximum) at room temperature.




 WARNING:
 Working with concentrated acids is dangerous and should be avoided!
 To remove calcium build-up, use standard acids only (e.g. vinegar).


씰 While cleaning, keep mechanical stress on mouldings, e.g. squeezing, to a minimum to prevent distortion.

씰 Water used for cleaning should be softened to prevent scaling. Calcium build-up can be removed with standard household
 acids, e.g. Vinegar.




19.3 Cleaning Acrylic Glass Windows


 CAUTION:
  Never use a car wash to clean the mobile home as the acrylic glass windows can
 be scratched by the rotating brushes!
  The acrylic glass windows must not be cleaned using chemical cleaners, glass
 cleaners or products that contain alcohol spirit. Cleaning products must not
 contain plasticizer as this would cause the acrylic glass windows to become brittle
 and crack!
  Avoid twisting and distorting the windows when opening and closing them.




 SUN-TRAVELLER
19-4 MJ 2006 • 10.2005 SPORT-TRAVELLER

title: CLEANING & CARE 씰 The acrylic glass windows should be cleaned using plenty of water with a clean sponge and a soft cloth
text:
CLEANING & CARE




씰 The acrylic glass windows should be cleaned using plenty of water with a clean sponge and a soft cloth.

씰 The only liquid which you should allow to come into contact with the acrylic glass is water with the addition of a 10 %
 solution of mild washing-up liquid to keep the windows clear and free from static.

씰 To remove stubborn stains use a special acrylic glass cleaner that is available from your KNAUS dealer.

씰 Regularly lubricate all hinges, flaps and windows with acid-free battery terminal grease or gun oil (e.g. Ballistol) to ensure
 that moving parts continue to move freely and do not seize.




 NOTE:
 Because of the physical properties of acrylic glass, the colder of the two glass panes,
 usually the one on the outside, will mist up on the inside. This condensation will
 disappear when the air around the window is very dry, but this process of diffusion
 can be quite slow.




19.4 Care and Cleaning of the Roof Window


 CAUTION:
  Failure to follow the instructions for the care and cleaning of the roof window
 will void the manufacturer’s warranty!
  When washing the mobile home it is to be ensured that the water jet is not
 pointed at the roof window. There is an air gap around the glass dome and the
 frame (forced ventilation) through which the water could enter the mobile home.


씰 Never use aggressive cleaners (solvents/scouring agents).

씰 Only clean acrylic glass (glass dome) and frame elements with a damp cloth and mild soap solution, if necessary use a
 special cleaner (at your own risk). This special cleaner is available from your KNAUS dealer.
 Ensure that there is no water on the mechanical parts.

씰 Treat all rubber seals with talcum powder.

씰 The 4 ventilation grilles and light covers can be removed for cleaning.

씰 Clean cover and mosquito blind with a soft brush, if necessary with a damp cloth.




SUN-TRAVELLER
SPORT-TRAVELLER MJ 2006 • 10.2005 19-5

title: CLEANING & CARE 19.5 Care and Cleaning of the Sun Awning
text:
CLEANING & CARE




19.5 Care and Cleaning of the Sun Awning
씰 The tent fabric should be regularly cleaned with a sponge and clean water or PVC cleaner as dirt particles can otherwise
 quickly create a culture medium for the growth of mould and bacteria.




19.5.1 Dealing with Mould on the Awning
It does not take long for mould to appear on the fabric of the awning if it is not cleaned and aired regularly, e.g. if it is folded
up and stowed away when wet. Avoid the use of strong solvents in this case!




 WARNING:
  When applying fungicides you must use protective equipment such
 as safety glasses, mask and safety gloves, and ensure that the area
 is well ventilated!
  The directions for use in the leaflet enclosed with the fungicide
 must be precisely followed.


씰 Clean the affected area with a 3% caustic soda solution (available from any pharmacist).

씰 Allow the caustic soda solution to act, then thoroughly rinse the fabric and dry.

씰 Repeat this treatment at regular intervals.




19.5.2 Treatment of Stiff Zip Fasteners
The zip fasteners on the awning can sometimes be stiff when new.

씰 This can be cured by rubbing candle wax into the zips or spraying them with a silicone spray.




 SUN-TRAVELLER
19-6 MJ 2006 • 10.2005 SPORT-TRAVELLER

title: Section 20 MAINTENANCE & INSPECTION
text:
Section 20




MAINTENANCE & INSPECTION

title: MAINTENANCE & INSPECTION 20. MAINTENANCE & INSPECTION
text:
MAINTENANCE & INSPECTION




20. MAINTENANCE & INSPECTION

20.1 Servicing
For the sake of road safety your mobile home must be regularly and properly serviced, just like a car.




 CAUTION:
 墌 Carrying out maintenance work on your mobile home requires specialist skills
 and knowledge. This is why you should have your mobile home serviced only by
 an authorised service centre.
 墌 The service centre will stamp your Customer Service Record Book to confirm
 that the leak tests on all tanks and pipes, hoses etc. have been carried out.
 墌 If any parts need replacing, ensure that the manufacturer’s original spare parts
 are used.




 NOTE:
 For mobile homes that are not used very often, servicing should be carried out
 once a year and in plenty of time before the start of a journey.




20.2 Intervals for Statutory Testing and Exhaust Gas Test
Under Section 29 StVZO, mobile homes under 3.5 t registered in Germany must be tested every 2 years (by TÜV, DEKRA);
mobile homes over 3.5 t must be tested annually.

In Germany all vehicles must also undergo an exhaust gas test at
the intervals shown in the table opposite. The test sticker on the Engine Cat Maximum Inspection
front number plate will tell you when the next test is due (see also type total weight interval
Section 23.1 Statutory tests).
 Turbo Diesel Yes Over 3.5 tonnes Annually


 Turbo Diesel Yes Up to 3.5 tonnes Every 2 Years
 NOTE:
 The relevant regulations which Petrol engine Yes – Every 2 Years
 apply in other countries must be
 complied with!
 Intervals for the Exhaust Gas Test (Germany)




SUN-TRAVELLER
SPORT-TRAVELLER MJ 2006 • 10.2005 20-1

title: MAINTENANCE & INSPECTION The onboard gas system must be tested by a gas engineer every 2 years (see also Section 23.1 St
text:
MAINTENANCE & INSPECTION




The onboard gas system must be tested by a gas engineer every 2 years (see also Section 23.1 Statutory Tests).

The engineer must issue a gas test certificate certifying that the gas system has been tested. The gas test sticker will be found
near the rear number plate.




20.3 Superstructure maintenance plan
Like any other machine, your mobile home needs to be properly serviced from time to time. The scope and frequency of this
work will depend first and foremost on the conditions under which the vehicle is used and operated.

All servicing work requires specialist skills and knowledge. It is therefore essential for this work to be carried out by trained
specialists.

The following table only lists the service and maintenance work that is carried out on the body of the mobile home. Please
refer to the separate respective manual for maintenance and inspection measures by FIAT and RENAULT.

text:
TROUBLESHOOTING AND FAULT RECTIFICATION




21. TROUBLESHOOTING AND FAULT RECTIFICATION
씰 Troubleshooting should be carried out by reference to the following Troubleshooting table.

씰 If you are unable to deal with malfunctions yourself you should contact the Customer Service department at your local

 WARNING:
 For your own safety’s sake you are advised to have repairs to the chassis carried
 out by a qualified service centre, especially repairs and adjustments to the brake
 system.

 Do not operate electrical switches (e.g.
 ignition), naked flames and smoking are
 strictly forbidden!

title: TROUBLESHOOTING AND FAULT RECTIFICATION HEATING / HOT WATER BOILER (ALDE HEATING)
text:
TROUBLESHOOTING AND FAULT RECTIFICATION




 HEATING / HOT WATER BOILER (ALDE HEATING)

 Fault Cause Action

 Heating will not start. No gas. Connect full gas bottle.

 Check that the gas bottle stop valve and
 appliance gas tap are open.

 If the heating was switched off for a long
 period of time or if the gas bottle was just
 connected it may take a bit longer until the
 gas circulates.

 Electrical connections loose. Check that electrical connections are tight
 on heating devices and connect properly.

 No voltage. Check whether voltage exists (> 11 V).

 Defective fuse. Fuse 13 A (230 V) or fuse 3 A (12 V) to be
 checked and replaced if necessary.

 Overheating protection triggered. If the overheating protection is triggered,
 the heating can only be put back into
 operation after it has cooled back down to
 10-20 °C.
 Check that the heating is well vented before
 switching on again.

 Red LED „Electronic controller blocked“ Switch heating off, wait approx. 30 seconds
 is illuminated. and switch the heating on again.
 If all previously specified measures do not
 solve the problem as required, a responsible
 technical specialist should be called.

 Electric cartridge does not function. No 230 Volt operating voltage. Ensure 230 Volt operating voltage.

 No 12 Volt operating voltage. Fuse in distribution box faulty.
 Fit new fuse.

 Battery monitor in distribution box faulty.
 Replace battery monitor.

 Faulty relay in heater. Check for whether the relay in the heater
 to be pulled (indicated by an audible click).
 If clicking can be heard, call a responsible
 technician.

 Vibrations in the circulating pump. Circulating pump is not properly Cap nut on the expansion tank is to be

 by foreign object. pump out of the expansion tank and

 Circulating pump will not start. Slider switch switched off on Alde control Switch on the slider switch, position „ ”,

 Temperature control is set lower than the This is not a circulating pump fault.
 actual room temperature. If the temperature in the mobile home
 drops lower than the set temperature on
 the temperature controller, the circulating
 pump switches on.

 Electrical connections loose. Check that electrical connections at
 circulating pump and make connections
 properly if necessary.

 No voltage. Check whether 12 V voltage exists.
 If all previously specified measures do not
 solve the problem technician should be
 called.

title: SPECIFICATIONS 22.2.2 Explanations on the Payload Calculation
text:
SPECIFICATIONS




22.2.2 Explanations on the Payload Calculation



 WARNING:
  The permitted total weight of the mobile home specified in the vehicle
 documentation must not be exceeded!
  An overloaded mobile home can go out of control while driving!
  Loading the mobile home is to be performed according to section 4.3.1!
  We recommend that you have the fully loaded vehicle weighed on a public
 weighbridge before commencing your journey.
  If the determined total weight of your mobile home has exceeded the permitted
 total weight, all guarantee claims to the manufacturer are voided and also
 insurance claims!


The payload is the difference between the permitted total weight and empty weight

EEC Directive 92/21/EEC governs the payload of mobile homes at European level. The regulations specified there closely
correspond with standard DIN EN 1646-2.




22.2.2.1 Empty weight
The mass of the vehicle in running order is the mass of the empty vehicle (including lubricants, toolkit, spare wheel, 90 % full
fuel tank), with factory-fitted standard equipment, plus the weight of the driver (75 kg) and the basic equipment (see
section 21.2.2.2).




22.2.2.2 Basic Equipment
The basic equipment comprises all items of equipment and fluids that are necessary for the safe and proper use of the mobile
home. It includes the weights of:

  Gas: Liquid gas bottles (up to 90% full)

  Water: Fresh water heater (100% full)
 Fresh water tank (90% full)




SUN-TRAVELLER
SPORT-TRAVELLER MJ 2006 • 10.2005 22-9

title: SPECIFICATIONS 22.2.2.3 Maximum total weight
text:
SPECIFICATIONS




22.2.2.3 Maximum total weight
This weight allows for specific operating conditions including factors such as material strength, load capacity of the tyres
etc. This weight must not be exceeded under any circumstances!




 NOTE:
 You will find the maximum technically permissible laden mass, the mass in running
 order and the payload in the specifications on pages 22-12 to 22-76.




22.2.3 Weights of the Payload

22.2.3.1 Weights of the Accessories
The accessories cover the weights of all objects that are available beyond the standard equipment. You will find the individual
weights in the list on pages 22-2 to 22-5, these are to be totalled at the end of the list.




22.2.3.2 Weights of the Personal Equipment
Personal equipment comprises the weights of all articles carried on the vehicle over and above the optional extras, such as:

  Persons carried (except driver)
  Pets
  Clothing and shoes
  Sanitary articles, toilet articles
  Kitchen articles, etc.
  Food
  Sports and leisure goods
  TV, radio, VCR etc.
  Toys

The individual weights for the personal equipment carried must be accurately determined and recorded in full in the list on
pages 22-6 to 22-8 and totalled.




 SUN-TRAVELLER
22-10 MJ 2006 • 10.2005 SPORT-TRAVELLER

title: SPECIFICATIONS 22.2.4 Formula for the payload calculation
text:
SPECIFICATIONS




22.2.4 Formula for the payload calculation
The payload is calculated as using the following formula: Formula e.g. Sun-Traveller 550 D


 Maximum total weight 3.500,00 kg
 - Empty weight = - 2.878,00 kg

 Payload 622,00 kg




 NOTE:
 The payload, i.e. accessories + personal equipment must not exceed a weight of
 622.00 kg in this example with the Sun-Traveller 550 D!




 NOTE:
  The particulars given in the vehicle document are binding for the specifications.
  You will find further information on the basic vehicle in the separate user manual
 supplied by the manufacturer.
  Other specifications are not part of this manual. These figures are enclosed
 with the mobile home as a separate manual.

title: Section 24 CHECKLIST
text:
Section 24




CHECKLIST

title: CHECKLIST 24. CHECKLIST
text:
CHECKLIST




24. CHECKLIST

 Comments: Date:




 1: Do you have all vehicle papers – including the green card – onboard, and do all passengers have their travel documents
 (passport, identity card, child's identity card, etc.)? Check validity of travel documents in good time! OK: ❑

 2: Do you have all necessary documents (health related documentation and vaccination certificate) for any pets carried?
 Information on the necessary documents can be obtained from the related consulate, from the tourist office or from an OK: ❑

 3: Is the gas test certificate still valid (see gas test sticker)? Check validity in good time! OK: ❑

 4: Do you have a complete set of spare keys (store separately) for the mobile home and any alarm? OK: ❑

 5: Have all the necessary servicing work and inspections been performed on the mobile home at the correct intervals? OK: ❑

 6: Has the permitted total weight of the mobile home been exceeded (see section 22.3)? OK: ❑

 7: Was loading the mobile home performed according to specifications (see section 4.3)? OK: ❑

 8: Are all doors, flaps, gas locker, windows, roof lights and REMI – except forced air vents – correctly closed? OK: ❑

 9: Have all hanging lights been removed and stored properly? OK: ❑

 10: Are all tables in sleeping position (see section Sleeping)? OK: ❑

 11: Are you sure that all containers with liquids – including those in the refrigerateur – cannot tip over? OK: ❑

 12: Are the gas bottles securely fastened with fastening straps in the gas locker, are the gas bottle stop valve and all gas taps
 closed? OK: ❑

 13: Is the payload securely fastened against sliding? OK: ❑

 14: Have all the tyre pressures (including the spare tyre) on the mobile home been checked as per section 22.1 Tyre Pressure
 Table? OK: ❑

 15: Are first-aid-kit (check for completeness!) and hazard warning triangle slowed where they are easy to reach? OK: ❑

 16: Do you have a correctly pumped up spare wheel, the corresponding tools and a suitable vehicle jack for the maximum
 technically permissible laden mass in case you need to change a wheel? OK: ❑

 17: Do all lights work on the mobile home (test!)? OK: ❑
 Copy if necessary
